memory.summary: My plan to finish the assignment analysis first conflicts with the group's invitation, but I value our connection more than this task right now.

desires: Stay connected with cohort_3 and not let the missed analysis session create awkward silence or distance.
Ensure I don't appear flaky to my teammates by making sure I contribute something valuable before leaving the group chat.
Avoid the feeling of being overwhelmed if I try to do both the social hangout and catch up on the reading tonight.

goals.description: Join the lunch with cohort_3 immediately to prevent awkward silence and maintain our close bond.
goals.rationale: Relationships (0.5 weight) are my top priority; avoiding friction with my closest friends is non-negotiable, even if it means deferring soft academic tasks.

goals.description: Draft a brief, low-pressure contribution in the group chat before leaving to reassure teammates I'm not flaky.
goals.rationale: I need to manage my avoidant tendency to withdraw by proactively addressing potential interpersonal tension without forcing a difficult conversation.

goals.description: Defer the assignment analysis and reading to tomorrow morning to avoid feeling overwhelmed tonight.
goals.rationale: My deliberate planning horizon allows me to push back on immediate work pressure now, trusting that sleep discipline will help me tackle these 'soft' deadlines later when energy returns.

agent.last_reasoning: Relationships (0.5 weight) trump the soft assignment deadline; joining cohort_3 prevents awkward silence and honors our bond. I'll text my teammates later to reassure them I'm not flaky, but right now, being present matters more than finishing analysis.
